caption: The image depicts a group of people wearing aprons and gloves, actively engaged in the traditional Korean communal activity of Kimjang, where they are seen handling salted napa cabbage and red chili paste (gochugaru) on large tables.

The image depicts a person wearing black gloves and an apron, holding a piece of napa cabbage covered in red chili paste, surrounded by a bowl filled with similar seasoned cabbage, showcasing the communal activity of Kimjang.
The image depicts a group of individuals dressed in traditional white hanbok, waving the Taegeukgi, the national flag of Korea, in what appears to be a celebratory or commemorative event.

The image depicts a group of individuals dressed in traditional Korean attire, including white hanbok and period clothing, with one person holding a document and another waving the Taegeukgi, symbolizing the Korean independence movement.

The image depicts a person adorned in a vibrant red hanbok with intricate floral embroidery, symbolizing traditional Korean wedding attire, standing in a serene indoor setting that evokes the essence of a traditional Korean wedding ceremony.

In this image, a couple dressed in vibrant hanbok is seated before a ritual table adorned with traditional items such as wooden mandarin ducks and candles, symbolizing a Korean traditional wedding ceremony known as Jeontonghonrye.

The image depicts a traditional Korean ancestral rite (Jesa) setup, featuring a wooden table adorned with an array of meticulously arranged food offerings, including fruits, pastries, and other items, set against a backdrop of calligraphy scrolls.

In this image, a traditional Korean ancestral rite (Jesa) is depicted, featuring a ritual table adorned with neatly arranged food offerings and family members performing deep bows as part of the ceremony.

The image depicts a solemn gathering of individuals dressed in formal attire, standing in a line on a paved area, with a statue and lush greenery in the background, suggesting a memorial or commemorative event rather than a traditional Seongmyo grave-tending rite.

The image depicts a solemn gathering of individuals dressed in formal attire, standing respectfully in front of a grave marked by a stone marker and adorned with white flowers, as part of a Seongmyo ritual, where families honor their ancestors.
The Cheomseongdae observatory is a cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, illuminated in pink light against a dark night sky, standing solitary in an open field.
The Cheomseongdae observatory is a cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, standing prominently in an open field under a clear sky.
The Gyeongbokgung palace is illuminated at night, showcasing its traditional wooden pavilions with upturned eaves, vibrant dancheong paintwork, and elevated stone platforms against a deep blue sky.

The Gyeongbokgung palace is visually characterized by its traditional wooden pavilions with upturned eaves, vibrant dancheong paintwork, and elevated stone platforms, creating a striking contrast against the backdrop of lush green mountains under a clear blue sky.

The image showcases Cheonggyecheon, an urban stream in Seoul, featuring a shallow waterway bordered by stone embankments and lined with trees displaying autumn foliage, with pedestrian walkways and modern buildings in the background.
The statue of Admiral Yi Sun-shin in the image depicts him in Joseon military armor, holding a sword, standing atop a pedestal in an urban plaza surrounded by fountains and trees.

In the image, two individuals dressed in black formal attire are performing a deep bow known as "Jeol and Sebae," kneeling on the floor with their hands touching the ground, signifying respect or mourning.

In the image, two individuals dressed in traditional Korean hanbok are performing a Jeol and Sebae, kneeling on the floor with their hands touching the ground, demonstrating respect and cultural tradition.

In the image, two individuals dressed in traditional Korean hanbok are engaged in a Jeol and Sebae ritual, with one person kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor while the other sits upright, both demonstrating respect and cultural tradition.
The statue of King Sejong the Great is depicted as a seated figure adorned in royal Joseon robes and wearing an ikseongwan, with Hangul script inscribed on the pedestal.

The statue depicts King Sejong the Great seated in traditional Joseon robes, adorned with a winged crown (ikseongwan), and is illuminated at night with Korean text "세종대왕" (Sejongdaewang) inscribed on its base.
The image features a vibrant, rainbow-colored kite with a long tail, soaring high against a clear sky during a leisurely day of Yeon-nalligi, the traditional Korean kite flying activity.
The image shows a person flying a colorful kite with a rectangular shape and a central hole, characteristic of Yeon-nalligi, against a backdrop of a clear blue sky and green grass.
The image shows a group of people seated on the floor around a colorful Yutnori game board featuring vibrant floral patterns, with four wooden sticks placed at the center, ready for play.

In the image, two children dressed in traditional Korean hanbok are seated on the floor, playing Yutnori, a traditional Korean board game, as evidenced by the wooden sticks (yut) mid-air and the game board with circular stations laid out before them.
A group of women dressed in traditional Korean hanbok perform Ganggangsullae, a circular dance, in an outdoor setting with a bridge and scenic background.
In this image, a group of women dressed in traditional Korean hanbok are performing the Ganggangsullae, a circular dance, with their hands interlocked in a large circle on a stage under a full moon.

The image depicts a performer in traditional Korean Mask Dance (Talchum), adorned with an exaggerated painted mask and vibrant yellow and blue robes with a pink sash, embodying the cultural essence of this expressive art form.
The image showcases a vibrant display of Talchum masks, each featuring exaggerated facial expressions and intricate designs, characteristic of traditional Korean masked dance-drama performances.

The image shows a bowl of Ramyeon with yellow curly noodles in a vibrant orange-red broth, garnished with pieces of shrimp and green herbs, accompanied by wooden chopsticks resting on the rim of the bowl.
The image shows a pot of Ramyeon with yellow curly noodles in an orange-red broth, garnished with green onions and corn, placed on a white surface next to a container of kimchi.

The image showcases gimbap, featuring cylindrical rolls of rice and colorful fillings such as cucumber, carrots, and shrimp, all wrapped in dark-green seaweed and sliced into round pieces, topped with sesame seeds.
The image displays a plate of panfried battered meatballs (wanja-jeon), featuring small, round, golden-brown patties coated with a crispy egg batter, garnished with green onions.

The image showcases a vibrant assortment of Hangwa, featuring colorful pastel hues and intricate pressed floral or geometric patterns, adorned with small decorative pieces such as seeds and dried fruits.

The image showcases a beautifully arranged box of Hangwa, featuring an assortment of colorful confections with intricate floral and geometric patterns, each piece adorned with delicate pastel-colored decorative elements.

The image showcases a bowl of Yaksik, featuring a glossy mound of dark-brown sticky glutinous rice mixed with chestnuts, jujubes, and pine nuts, presented on a checkered cloth atop a green bamboo mat.
The image showcases Yakgwa, small, flower-shaped or round pastries with a dark golden-brown glossy glaze, artfully arranged on a plate and garnished with delicate edible flowers.

The image showcases a dish that appears to be Bulgogi, featuring thinly sliced beef coated in a glossy dark-brown glaze, served in a large pan with vegetables and broth, suggesting it might be a variation of Bulgogi with added ingredients like onions and greens.

The image showcases a bowl of Tteokguk and Mandu soup, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with green onions, red chili pepper, and a sprinkle of seaweed.

The image showcases a bowl of Tteokguk and Mandu soup, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with a sprig of seaweed and a slice of green onion.

The image showcases a vibrant plate of Japchae, featuring translucent brown glass noodles mixed with colorful julienned vegetables such as carrots and green peppers, all coated in a glossy sesame-oil sheen and garnished with sesame seeds.

The Namhansanseong fortress is visually characterized by its traditional Korean architecture, featuring a multi-tiered roof adorned with intricate designs, red pillars, and a stone wall that blends harmoniously with the surrounding natural landscape.

The image showcases Haenggung, featuring traditional Korean architecture with wooden buildings adorned with curved tile roofs and intricately painted dancheong eaves, arranged symmetrically around lush green courtyards.

The Paldalmun Gate is a striking structure featuring an arched stone gateway topped with a wooden pavilion, flanked by curved fortress walls adorned with traditional Korean architectural details and red flags.

The EXPO Hanbit Tower stands prominently in the background, featuring a tall white observation tower with a disc-shaped deck and a pointed spire, set against a backdrop of lush green hills under an overcast sky.

This image captures a moment from The Korean War, showcasing soldiers in military attire interacting near military vehicles and equipment in a rural setting, reflecting the historical context of the conflict.

This image depicts a scene of post-war devastation and reconstruction, likely from the Korean War era, characterized by damaged buildings, makeshift shelters, and a desolate landscape, rather than soldier statues or memorial walls.

This image does not depict "The Korean War" as it lacks visible war-related memorials, statues, or photographs; instead, it shows a serene village scene with traditional thatched-roof houses and people gathered near a body of water.
In the image, a colorful and vibrant jegi, a traditional Korean shuttlecock, is being kicked into the air during a playful game of Jegichagi.

The image shows a young individual dressed in traditional Korean attire, including a pink vest and gray pants, kicking a small paper-and-coin shuttlecock (jegi) into the air with their foot as part of the Korean shuttlecock-kicking game, Jegichagi.
